name: codex-frontier
description: Stop-hook with two end-of-turn jobs, both via a DO:MON consult. (1) WORK FRONTIER: when the turn left open non-[USER] items, apply: 1. Finish the requested objective. 2. Classify discovered work. 3. Immediately drain the discovered-work frontier when it is safe, relevant, and tool-executable. 4. Stop only when the frontier contains no worthwhile safe work, or only user-owned/irreversible decisions remain. Clean up agent-owned processes before stopping; terminate dev servers, watchers, worker pools, hook/helper shells, and background toolchain commands you started, but do not kill shared or user-owned processes unless the user explicitly authorizes it. Hard architecture, design, outward-impact, acceptance-criteria, tradeoff, or scalability decisions are surfaced as [DO:MON], not [USER]; the agent consults do:mon with code/evidence and asks for code, ideas, definition of done, acceptance criteria, tradeoffs, and the long-term scalable solution, then verifies and continues. Agent-created rollout / flip / readiness gates are frontier work, not terminal user decisions. Execution loop: objective -> required fixes -> verification -> discovered frontier -> drain -> verify -> stop. (2) ADR / SPEC ALIGNMENT: when the turn changed code AND the project has a registered ADR (docs/adr/) or grounded-docs specification index, review the change against it per @docscheck and, on divergence, ALIGN it -- DO:MON reviews + proposes the fix (apply it), with do:distinguished-engineer (implementation) / do:test-engineer (tests) as fallbacks; codex --decide remains a fallback when DO:MON is unavailable. Blocks once; self-gates on the do.manifest; recursion-safe; decline-respecting; the alignment branch self-suppresses where no ADR/spec is registered; FAIL-OPEN throughout. Replaces the old local codex-on-question hooks, which fired on any '?'. DISCLOSURE: the consult it asks for sends the (secret-scrubbed) frontier items / changed-code context to an EXTERNAL LLM via the do:mon skill; the hook itself emits only a directive and egresses nothing.

disclosure.target: ChatGPT / external reasoner via the do:mon skill
disclosure.dataSent: the open non-[USER] frontier items and turn context, ONLY when Claude runs the do:mon skill the directive asks for; the hook itself sends nothing. The do:mon skill secret-scrubs before egress.
disclosure.execution: advisory by default -- the hook never runs ChatGPT or codex; Claude invokes the do:mon skill as a normal, visible tool call. Codex remains only a fallback for codex --decide or explicit ASK_CODEX_ALLOW_EDITS=1 direct-edit flows.
